name: Acceldata Data Observability API
what_it_does: Enterprise data observability platform with REST API — providing unified monitoring of data pipelines, data quality checks, system reliability, and cost optimization across multi-cloud and on-premises data stacks, with root cause analysis and ML-based anomaly detection.
use_cases: Monitoring data pipeline health and data quality across Spark, Airflow, and warehouses via API
Querying data quality check results and anomaly alerts for incident management integration
Pulling pipeline performance metrics for cost optimization and capacity planning
Integrating Acceldata alerts with PagerDuty or Slack for data team on-call workflows
Automating data quality rule management across multiple environments via API
not_for: Lightweight single-warehouse data quality (use Soda or Monte Carlo for simpler needs)
ETL orchestration and pipeline building (use dbt or Airflow)
Open-source self-hosted monitoring without enterprise support
best_when: A large enterprise data team needs observability across heterogeneous data infrastructure (on-prem + cloud, multiple engines) with unified visibility and enterprise SLA requirements.
avoid_when: You have a simple, cloud-native data stack (use Bigeye or Monte Carlo), or need open-source self-hosted observability.

auth.notes: API token from Acceldata tenant. Bearer token auth. Tenant-scoped access (multi-tenant enterprise deployment). Webhook for alert and anomaly events. No OAuth2. Enterprise deployment — endpoint varies per customer tenant.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; proof of concept available
pricing.notes: Indian company (Bangalore/San Jose). Founded 2018. Raised $55M. Series B company. Strong in financial services, healthcare, and retail with complex data infrastructures. On-prem and cloud-native deployments. Acceldata Pulse for system observability, Torch for data quality. Multi-engine support: Spark, Kafka, Hadoop, Hive, Presto.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for pipelines, data quality checks, alerts, and metrics. Bearer token auth. Enterprise documentation with tenant-specific endpoint. Multi-product platform (Pulse, Torch) — API may differ per product. Data quality rules and anomaly thresholds configurable. Webhook for real-time alerting.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert reads fully idempotent. Data quality check reads idempotent. Pipeline metric reads idempotent by time range. Rule configuration reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Enterprise deployment — API endpoint is tenant-specific, not a shared cloud URL
Acceldata has multiple products (Pulse, Torch) — clarify which API to use
Agent deployment required on compute clusters before monitoring begins
Data quality rules require configuration before check execution is possible
Complex multi-engine deployments may have different metric structures per engine

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. HIPAA available. Multi-region data residency. Bearer token auth with tenant isolation. Data observability metadata is sensitive — enterprise security standards. Agent requires access to production data infrastructure.
